summary.body: Computer Engineering undergraduate at UFS with experience in full-stack development, test automation, CI/CD, and applied research. I build software with focus on maintainability, delivery quality, and measurable impact, combining product validation with engineering practices.

bullet_points: Built a React Native (Expo) and Spring Boot mobile MVP for pilot launch, delivering five core flows: authentication, campaign creation, donations, donation history, and voucher creation/recovery.
Automated regression validation across core product flows with Maestro and WireMock, covering 63 end-to-end scenarios and improving release stability and troubleshooting efficiency.
Improved payment-flow reliability and system resilience by integrating multiple payment providers, removing dependency on a single provider and reducing operational risk.
Accelerated releases and deployment reliability by implementing CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Terraform-managed AWS infrastructure, reducing a 1-hour manual process to a 15-minute automated pipeline.

Built a plagiarism-detection evaluation dataset for reliable benchmarking, comprising 500 original source codes (250 in C and 250 in Python) plus obfuscated versions generated through dataset modeling and open-source obfuscation tools.

Leveraged this dataset to implement a Python-based Tree-sitter converter that generates reversible syntax-tree JSON representations for automated code comparison, achieving same-language plagiarism-detection F1-scores of 86.36% for Python and 68.63% for C.

Won 2nd place at the ChipLabs Hackathon for building an LLM routing module for multiple AI APIs using LangChain and PostgreSQL as a vector database, reducing inference cost by routing prompts to the smallest adequate model instead of a fixed default.
Achieved 280th place out of 984 teams in the SBC 2025 Programming Marathon (first phase) by coaching the team through training and qualification.
